-- 6 of 143 --

REMEMBER...
• When it comes to SELLING your product, the absolute most
important thing you have to talk about and sell is the
TRANSFORMATION... not the way the product is delivered
• HOWEVER, after you make the sale you have to deliver the product.
That’s what this Module is all about...
• Doing a great job on your product delivery is a lot easier than you
think... but there’s a bunch of things you have to get right, and there
are some big mistakes to avoid. I’m going to walk you through them in
this module...

How to format your calls...
• Split your subject into five general topic areas (or four +1)

-- 48 of 143 --

How to format your calls...
• Split your subject into five general topic areas (or four +1)
• Which topic should go first?
• Which should go second, third, fourth, and fifth?
• First call should have a general introduction for the entire course
• Last call should have a general conclusion and “looking forward” for
the entire course

-- 59 of 143 --

How to Structure Each Call
• Start with the WHY... tell them why this topic is important
• Then cover the WHAT... what are you actually talking about
• Then teach the HOW TO... the nitty-gritty stuff about how to actually
do it
Wednesday, June 5, 2013
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 60 of 143 --

How to Structure Each Call
• Start with the WHY... tell them why this topic is important
• Then cover the WHAT... what are you actually talking about
• Then teach the HOW TO... the nitty-gritty stuff about how to actually
do it
• Now take all those key points, questions, mistakes, objections and put
them into that overall structure... why, what, how to
Wednesday, June 5, 2013
www.getwsodownload.com

2. Recordings
• Most services will do this automatically for you
• It’s good to have a backup...
• BACKUP: If you’re doing teleseminars, you can record the audio
locally using a smartphone or audio recorder
• BACKUP: If you’re doing webinars, use Camtasia or Screenflow on a
separate computer
• If everything fails, you can always do the call again

3. Transcripts
• Don’t need to do this
• But it adds a lot of value for both your clients AND you
• Typical cost is about $30-50 per hour of audio recording
• Remember version 1.0 vs polished
• For me, an hour of audio will generate 18-25 pages of content.You
might speak at a different rate of speed.
• Be sure to use someone that actually knows how to transcribe stuff
• We’ve used InternetTranscribers.com extensively

4. Surveys
• Before/After each call
• I’ve used SurveyMonkey.com for years
• Other options: Survey Gizmo and Google Docs
• When creating a survey... keep it as short as possible
• Use a redirect or URL shortener (i.e., something like bit.ly)

What to ask on your surveys...
• Ask these two questions:
• What questions do you still have about the last topic
• What questions to you have about the next topic
• And also ask for name and email address, in case you want to
follow up with them
